MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jun-26-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Atty. J. Kevin Morrison of the Altair Law, tel. No. 1-415-988-9828 appearing for the
Plaintiff;
Atty. Jay M. Bulger of the Koeller, Nebeker halluck LLP, Tel. No. 1-619-233-1600
appearing for the Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D; Defendants Bosa Development California, Inc. and Bosa Development California II, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment on all claims alleged against them by plaintiff Gilberto Robles Vasquez complaint is granted as to both defendants. The undisputed facts show that Bosa CA did not direct the manner of the work performed by E&S and did not directly participate in that work. A "hirer's failure to correct an unsafe condition, by itself, does not establish an affirmative contribution." (Khosh v. Staples Construction Company, Inc. (2016) 4 Cal.App.5th 712, 718). Ray v. Silverado Constructors (2002) 98 Cal.App.4th 1120 is distinguishable because the general contractor in that case had exclusive control over the particular hazard, unlike here where control and responsibility for safety was shared as evidenced by the subcontract. There is no evidence that Bosa CA made a promise "to undertake a particular safety measure" or negligently failed to perform "that specific promise." (Khosh) Srithong v. Total Investment Co. (1994) 23 Cal.App.4th 721 is inapposite because that case did not discuss the Privette doctrine as related to the liability of a hirer for injury to a worker employed by the contractor and focused on a third party leasing part of the owner's premises. The concealed hazard exception is not applicable because the improperly secured opening was not a pre-existing concealed hazard such that E&S could not have reasonably known about it through an inspection. The unsecured plywood covering the hole was marked "hole" with orange spray and there are no facts from which a reasonable trier of fact could infer that an inspection would not reasonably ascertain it. (Kinsman v. Unocal Corp. (2005) 37 Cal.4th 659, 677 ("the principles enunciated in Privette suggest that the landowner would not be liable when the contractor has failed to engage in inspections of the premises implicitly or explicitly delegated to it.")). Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR # 8600 =302/hk
